The study of the scattering of high energy electrons from nuclei has yielded much interesting information about the charge distributions in nuclei and nucleons. We shall here consider a simple version in which the electrons are supposed to have zero spin. We also assume that the nucleus, of charge $Z e$, remains fixed in space (i.e., its mass is assumed infinite). Let $\rho(\mathbf{x})$ denote the charge density in the nucleus. Let $f\left(\mathbf{p}_{i}, \mathbf{p}_{f}\right)$ be the scattering amplitude of an electron from a real nucleus of the same charge. Let $\mathbf{q}=\mathbf{p}_{i}-\mathbf{p}_{f}$ denote the momentum transfer. The quantity $F$ defined by
$$ f\left(\mathbf{p}_{i}, \mathbf{p}_{f}\right)=F\left(q^{2}\right) f_{c}\left(\mathbf{p}_{i}, \mathbf{p}_{f}\right) $$
is called the form factor. It is easily seen that F , in fact, depends on $\mathbf{p}_{i}$ and $\mathbf{p}_{f}$ only through the quantity $q^{2}$.
